blanks.sentence: A network of online communities and influencers promoting certain ideas about _____
Key values promoted include emotional control, material wealth and _____ over women
Spreads through platforms such as social media posts and _____
Content targets a _____ audience, sometimes causing harm
Source of statistics: A _____ called Movember Foundation
Finding: _____ of British young men regularly follow masculinity influencers online
Core belief: the world is _____ men
True masculinity means being strong, wealthy and _____ others
Stood out from peers: enjoyed ballet, acting and _____
His differences made him an easy target — someone _____ and easily exploited
By age _____, he was already displaying bravado in photos
Felt pressured to follow the 'man box' — a _____ of ideals and rules
Could not maintain a relationship or _____ a job
Believed his personal problems were _____ women
Influencers told him the world _____ men
This _____ his worldview, making it unrealistic
He began viewing society as _____ — men against everyone else
Saw women, progressives and the _____ as opponents trying to reduce men's power
His mental health worsened until he reached _____
Now travels to schools across the UK, encouraging young people to _____ what they encounter online

multipleChoice.question: According to Anita Rani, which of the following best describes what the manosphere promotes as indicators of a man's value?
options: Financial independence, physical fitness, emotional openness, and leadership
Emotional restraint, material success, physical looks, and control over others
Social influence, intellectual ability, career achievement, and self-discipline
Psychological strength, online presence, competitive drive, and peer respect

multipleChoice.question: What does Phil suggest about the way manosphere content reaches its audience?
options: It is distributed through a dedicated website targeting vulnerable teenagers
It is broadcast via mainstream television programmes aimed at young adults
It is shared through official men's organisations with structured membership
It spreads through social media platforms and online videos without a central hub

multipleChoice.question: Why did Will Adolphy consider himself particularly susceptible to manosphere ideas during his teenage years?
options: His unconventional interests set him apart and left him open to being exploited
His academic struggles made him seek validation from online communities instead
His lack of male role models pushed him towards influencers for life guidance
His desire for fame as an actor made him vulnerable to social media manipulation

multipleChoice.question: In the context of Will's behaviour at age 14, what does his use of "bravado" most closely suggest?
options: He genuinely developed confidence after receiving encouragement from his peers
He openly rejected social expectations and embraced his artistic identity proudly
He projected a false image of toughness that concealed his actual inner struggles
He consciously chose to follow masculine ideals after careful personal reflection

multipleChoice.question: What was Will's emotional state when he first encountered manosphere influencers online?
options: He was sceptical of their claims but continued watching out of intellectual curiosity
He was receptive because their messages seemed to confirm his real-life frustrations
He was indifferent at first but gradually became convinced by repeated exposure
He was enthusiastic about self-improvement and sought out motivational content

multipleChoice.question: According to Will, what impact did prolonged exposure to manosphere content have on his perception of reality?
options: It warped his understanding of the world into a distorted and adversarial perspective
It motivated him to improve himself by addressing his personal weaknesses directly
It gradually helped him identify the social injustices that men genuinely experience
It encouraged him to distance himself from toxic relationships in his personal life

multipleChoice.question: When Phil explains that Will "painted everyone with the same brush," what does this idiom imply about Will's thinking?
options: Will carefully analysed each person's behaviour before forming a negative judgement
Will expressed his frustration through creative outlets such as writing and painting
Will selectively targeted specific groups he believed were responsible for his problems
Will unfairly attributed the same negative traits to all people based on a few examples

multipleChoice.question: Based on the description of Will's "us versus them" mindset, what can be inferred about how he viewed women and the mainstream media?
options: He regarded them as misguided but ultimately willing to understand men's struggles
He saw them as irrelevant to his personal difficulties and largely ignored their views
He perceived them as active opponents deliberately working to diminish male influence
He considered them to be victims of the same social pressures that affected young men

multipleChoice.question: What can be inferred about the circumstances under which Will eventually disengaged from the manosphere?
options: He left after a trusted mentor helped him critically evaluate the content he consumed
He withdrew voluntarily once he recognised the harmful nature of the ideology himself
He was guided out by a school counsellor who intervened during his teenage years
His departure came only after his mental wellbeing had already seriously deteriorated

multipleChoice.question: What is Will Adolphy's primary purpose in visiting schools across the UK after his recovery?
options: To warn educators about the dangers of unrestricted internet access for young people
To encourage young people to maintain a critical and questioning attitude toward online content
To promote mental health awareness by sharing clinical research on social media addiction
To advocate for government regulation of influencer content on social media platforms
